commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a session-file-scoped embedded prompt holder registry in the agent session-lock controller and a regression test for two prompt holders sharing one session JSONL file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current `attempt.ts` calls `releaseForPrompt()` before compaction wait and later reacquires via `withSessionWriteLock()`, while the patch only clears the new holder from `reacquireAfterPrompt()`.
commands.review_followup: There is a narrow, mechanical blocker in the session-lock controller that an automated repair pass can attempt without a product decision.

target.title: fix(agents): guard session prompt ownership
target.body:
## Summary
- serialize embedded runner prompt releases by resolved session JSONL file
- reacquire a write lock before a second provider can stream into a prompt-active transcript
- add a regression test for duplicate prompt holders sharing the same session file

Fixes #85913

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/logging/diagnostic-stuck-session-recovery.runtime.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/failover-error.test.ts`
  - Passed: 7 files, 298 tests, Duration 52.27s.
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: duplicate embedded attempts for the same resolved session JSONL file could both release their write locks for provider prompt streaming at the same time, allowing overlapping prompt-active transcript ownership.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node v24.15.0, branch `fix/session-file-prompt-guard-85913`, using the real `acquireSessionWriteLock` implementation against a temporary session JSONL file.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with an inline runtime script that created two `createEmbeddedAttemptSessionLockController` instances for the same temp `session.jsonl`, called `first.releaseForPrompt()`, started `second.releaseForPrompt()`, waited 50 ms, then called `first.reacquireAfterPrompt()` and `first.acquireForCleanup().release()` before awaiting the second release.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live terminal output from that command:

```json
{
  "node": "v24.15.0",
  "platform": "darwin/arm64",
  "sessionFile": "<temp>/session.jsonl",
  "events": [
    "first released for prompt",
    "second released before first reacquire: false",
    "first reacquired after prompt",
    "first cleanup released",
    "second released for prompt",
    "takeover first=false second=false"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: while the first controller still owned the prompt-active session file, `secondReleasedForPrompt` remained `false`; only after the first controller reacquired and released cleanup did the second controller release for prompt. Both controllers reported `hasSessionTakeover() === false`.
- What was not tested: no additional gaps.

## Authorship
Please preserve author attribution if this PR is squashed or reworked, or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`
target.author: turbotheturtle
